cluster_ids.name: On/Off
cluster_ids.summary: Controls a device’s on/off state; features LT/DF/OFFONLY tune behavior. Pseudocode: if cmd==OFF → OnOff=false; if cmd==ON → OnOff=true.

classifications.summary: Base application cluster on an endpoint. Pseudocode: Endpoint[OnOff] implements core on/off app behavior.

revisions.description: Added mandatory global ClusterRevision attribute; CCB 1555
Added FeatureMap global attribute support with Level Control and Lighting feature
Adopted new data model format and notation
Added Dead Front behavior and associated FeatureMap entry
Added OffOnly feature and associated FeatureMap entry

features.name: Lighting
features.summary: Behavior for lighting applications; on LevelControl→OnOff=FALSE: OnTime:=0; on LevelControl→OnOff=TRUE and OnTime==0: OffWaitTime:=0.

features.name: DeadFrontBehavior
features.summary: When OnOff==FALSE, device enters 'dead front': other clusters may fail Invoke/Write with INVALID_IN_STATE and use best-effort attribute values; reporting/events continue.

features.name: OffOnly
features.summary: Device supports only Off command; On and Toggle commands are not supported, e.g. due to regulatory constraints.

values.name: AcceptOnlyWhenOn
values.summary: If OnOff == TRUE then accept command, else reject; command only valid when device is On

values.name: Off
values.summary: Set OnOff attribute to FALSE on startup

values.name: On
values.summary: Set OnOff attribute to TRUE on startup

values.name: Toggle
values.summary: If OnOff was FALSE set to TRUE; if TRUE set to FALSE (toggle on startup)

values.name: DelayedAllOff
values.summary: Selects the Delayed All Off effect variant

values.name: DyingLight
values.summary: Selects the Dying Light effect variant

values.name: NoFade
values.summary: Turn off immediately with no fade

values.name: DelayedOffSlowFade
values.summary: Dim to 50% in 0.8s then fade to off in 12s

values.name: DyingLightFadeOff
values.summary: Dim up 20% in 0.5s then fade to off in 1s

attributes.name: OnOff
attributes.summary: Indicates whether the device is off (FALSE) or on (TRUE). Updated by commands like Off/On/Toggle and others that change power state.

attributes.name: GlobalSceneControl
attributes.summary: Controls storing/recall of global scene: set TRUE after commands that turn OnOff=TRUE; set FALSE after OffWithEffect to avoid overwriting or restoring all-off scene.

attributes.name: OnTime
attributes.summary: Time in 1/10 s to keep On state when using OnWithTimedOff; writable anytime but only effective in Timed On state.

attributes.name: OffWaitTime
attributes.summary: Time in 1/10 s guarding Off state from new OnWithTimedOff after Timed On → Delayed Off or in Delayed Off state.

attributes.name: StartUpOnOff
attributes.summary: Defines power-up behavior reflected in OnOff: if null, restore previous OnOff; else use behavior from StartUpOnOffEnum; OTA reboot restores prior OnOff.

commands.name: Off
commands.summary: Turn device off: set OnOff := FALSE; if OnTime supported then set OnTime := 0.
commands.effect_on_receipt: on_receipt(Off): set OnOff := FALSE; if supports(OnTime) then set OnTime := 0;

commands.name: On
commands.summary: Turn device on unless OffOnly feature; may clear OffWaitTime if OnTime == 0.
commands.effect_on_receipt: on_receipt(On): if feature(OffOnly) == TRUE then send_status(UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND); else set OnOff := TRUE; if supports(OnTime) and supports(OffWaitTime) then if OnTime == 0 then set OffWaitTime := 0; end if; end if;

commands.name: Toggle
commands.summary: Toggle OnOff; update OnTime/OffWaitTime depending on new OnOff state.
commands.effect_on_receipt: on_receipt(Toggle): if feature(OffOnly) == TRUE then send_status(UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND); else if OnOff == FALSE then set OnOff := TRUE; else set OnOff := FALSE; end if; if supports(OnTime) and supports(OffWaitTime) then if OnOff == FALSE and OnTime == 0 then set OffWaitTime := 0; end if; if OnOff == TRUE then set OnTime := 0; end if; end if;

commands.name: OffWithEffect
commands.summary: Turn off with fading effect; may store global scene and manage GlobalSceneControl, OnTime, OnOff.

fields.name: EffectIdentifier
fields.summary: Select fading effect when turning off; must be a non-reserved enum value.

fields.name: EffectVariant
fields.constraint: non-reserved value of DelayedAllOffEffectVariantEnum or DyingLightEffectVariantEnum
fields.summary: Select variant of chosen effect; if unsupported by server, use default variant.

commands.effect_on_receipt: on_receipt(OffWithEffect, EffectIdentifier, EffectVariant): if GlobalSceneControl == TRUE then call store_global_scene(); set GlobalSceneControl := FALSE; set OnOff := FALSE; if supports(OnTime) then set OnTime := 0; end if; else set OnOff := FALSE; end if;

commands.name: OnWithRecallGlobalScene
commands.summary: Recall stored global scene if GlobalSceneControl == FALSE and then set GlobalSceneControl := TRUE; may clear OffWaitTime.
commands.effect_on_receipt: on_receipt(OnWithRecallGlobalScene): if GlobalSceneControl == TRUE then discard_command(); else ScenesManagementCluster.recall_global_scene(); // OnOff updated by Scenes cluster; set GlobalSceneControl := TRUE; end if; if supports(OnTime) and supports(OffWaitTime) then if OnTime == 0 then set OffWaitTime := 0; end if; end if;

commands.name: OnWithTimedOff
commands.summary: Turn on with timing guards: uses OnOffControl.AcceptOnlyWhenOn, OnTime, OffWaitTime and periodic 1/10s updates.
commands.timing: OnTime and OffWaitTime attributes updated every 1/10 s until both reach 0 (unless either is 0xFFFF).

fields.name: OnOffControl
fields.summary: Bitmap controlling processing; includes AcceptOnlyWhenOn bit which may require OnOff == TRUE to accept command.

fields.name: OnTime
fields.summary: Value used to adjust OnTime attribute; 0xFFFF disables automatic decrement.

fields.name: OffWaitTime
fields.summary: Value used to adjust OffWaitTime attribute; 0xFFFF disables automatic decrement.

commands.effect_on_receipt: on_receipt(OnWithTimedOff, OnOffControl, OnTimeField, OffWaitTimeField): if OnOffControl.AcceptOnlyWhenOn == 1 and OnOff == FALSE then discard_command(); return; end if; if OffWaitTime > 0 and OnOff == FALSE then set OffWaitTime := min(OffWaitTime, OffWaitTimeField); else set OnTime := max(OnTime, OnTimeField); set OffWaitTime := OffWaitTimeField; set OnOff := TRUE; end if; if OnTime != 0xFFFF and OffWaitTime != 0xFFFF then start_timer(0.1s, repeat): if OnOff == TRUE and OnTime > 0 then OnTime := OnTime - 1; if OnTime == 0 then OffWaitTime := 0; OnOff := FALSE; if OffWaitTime == 0 and OnTime == 0 then stop_timer(); end if; end if; else if OnOff == FALSE and OffWaitTime > 0 then OffWaitTime := OffWaitTime - 1; if OffWaitTime == 0 then stop_timer(); end if; end if; end if; end_timer_logic;

State Description.behavior: When OnWithTimedOff(x,y) received and OnOff is TRUE, OnTime is set to max(x, OnTime); OffWaitTime is set to y. If both OnTime and OffWaitTime are not 0xFFFF, the server updates them every 1/10s until both are 0: while OnOff=TRUE and OnTime>0, OnTime is decremented each tick. If OnTime reaches 0, OffWaitTime is set to 0 and OnOff is set to FALSE.

When OnWithTimedOff(x,y) received and OnOff is FALSE, OffWaitTime is set to min(y, OffWaitTime) per the state machine. If both OnTime and OffWaitTime are not 0xFFFF, the server updates them every 1/10s until both are 0: while OnOff=FALSE and OffWaitTime>0, OffWaitTime is decremented each tick. When OffWaitTime reaches 0, the server terminates the update.

references.name: FeatureMap
references.description: Bitmap of cluster features (LT, DF, OFFONLY) controlling behavior; defined elsewhere. Pseudocode: if FeatureMap&OFFONLY → restrict to off-only.

references.name: Level Control cluster
references.description: Referenced cluster whose commands affect OnOff, OnTime, OffWaitTime. Pseudocode: OnLevelCmd → update OnOff/OnTime/OffWaitTime.

references.name: OnOff attribute
references.description: Boolean attribute controlling on/off state; behavior described but full definition is elsewhere.

references.name: OnTime attribute
references.description: Timer attribute adjusted when Level Control changes OnOff; full semantics defined outside this section.

references.name: OffWaitTime attribute
references.description: Timer attribute set when OnTime==0 and On command received; full details defined elsewhere.

references.name: INVALID_IN_STATE status code
references.description: Interaction error for disallowed operations during dead-front; precise status definition is external.

references.name: ClusterRevision
references.description: Global attribute giving cluster version; value SHALL equal highest revision in table (not defined here)

references.name: StartUpOnOff
references.description: ZLO 1.0 behavior/attribute controlling on/off state at startup; details defined elsewhere

references.name: On/Off
references.description: Cluster for toggling devices; full attribute/command set specified outside revision section

references.name: Level Control Cluster
references.description: Cluster whose commands can change OnOff; when its command sets OnOff, OnTime and OffWaitTime are updated per Lighting feature rules.

references.name: OnTime
references.description: On/Off cluster attribute; set to 0 when Level Control command causes OnOff to become FALSE under Lighting feature.

references.name: OffWaitTime
references.description: On/Off cluster attribute; set to 0 when Level Control command causes OnOff TRUE and OnTime==0 under Lighting feature.

references.name: OnOff
references.description: Main boolean attribute of On/Off cluster; controls 'dead front' behavior and is changed by On/Off/Toggle and Level Control commands.

references.name: Off Command
references.description: On/Off cluster command that sets OnOff to FALSE and may trigger 'dead front' behavior or OffOnly operation.

references.name: On Command
references.description: On/Off cluster command that sets OnOff to TRUE; not supported when OffOnly feature is present.

references.name: Toggle Command
references.description: On/Off cluster command that toggles OnOff; not supported when OffOnly feature is present.

references.name: Report Transactions
references.description: Reporting interactions that continue during 'dead front' and may include best-effort attribute values.

references.name: INVALID_IN_STATE
references.description: Status code used by other clusters to fail Invoke/Write while device is in 'dead front' state.

references.name: OnOffControlBitmap
references.description: map8-derived type containing bits like AcceptOnlyWhenOn; referenced here but only its bit table is partially shown.

references.name: StartUpOnOffEnum
references.description: enum8-derived type controlling startup OnOff behavior; referenced but not detailed in this section.

references.name: Data Model Specification
references.description: Defines base types such as enum8, map8, bool, uint16 and general attribute semantics

references.name: Scenes Management server cluster
references.description: Provides Scene Table and ExtensionFieldSetStruct that may include OnOff attribute extensions

references.name: ExtensionFieldSetStruct
references.description: Structure in Scenes Management cluster whose fields may be extended with OnOff attribute

references.name: OnWithTimedOff command
references.description: Command that uses OnTime and OffWaitTime: maintains On for OnTime, then transitions to Delayed Off and guards Off for OffWaitTime.

references.name: OffWithEffect command
references.description: Command that turns device off with an effect and sets GlobalSceneControl attribute to FALSE so global scene is not overwritten.

references.name: OnWithRecallGlobalScene command
references.description: Command that sets OnOff to TRUE and recalls the stored global scene; sets GlobalSceneControl to TRUE after execution.

references.name: MoveToLevel(WithOnOff) command
references.description: Level control command that can set OnOff=TRUE and thereby set GlobalSceneControl=TRUE when turning device on.

references.name: RecallScene command
references.description: Scenes command that recalls a scene and may set OnOff=TRUE; causes GlobalSceneControl to be set to TRUE when it turns device on.

references.name: Global scene (group 0, scene 0)
references.description: Special scene stored with group ID 0 and scene ID 0 representing last setting when devices turned off; recalled on certain On operations.

references.name: GlobalSceneControl attribute
references.description: On/Off cluster attribute; controls whether a global scene is stored/recallable and toggled by OffWithEffect/OnWithRecallGlobalScene.

references.name: OffOnly feature
references.description: On/Off cluster feature flag; when supported, On and Toggle commands must respond with UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND.

references.name: Scenes Management cluster
references.description: Cluster on same endpoint; its server recall_global_scene() is invoked by OnWithRecallGlobalScene to restore settings.

references.name: EffectIdentifierEnum
references.description: Enum of fading effects for OffWithEffect; EffectIdentifier field must use non-reserved values.

references.name: DelayedAllOffEffectVariantEnum
references.description: Enum of variants for delayed-all-off effect; used to validate EffectVariant for certain EffectIdentifier values.

references.name: DyingLightEffectVariantEnum
references.description: Enum of variants for dying-light effect; used to validate EffectVariant for certain EffectIdentifier values.

references.name: UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND status
references.description: Interaction Model error status returned when On/Toggle are invoked on servers with OffOnly feature.
